title: PoW replay set: DashMap::retain acquires global write-lock on every auth attempt
description: ws.rs PoW verification runs pow_replay_set.retain(...) on every successful authentication (not on a timer). DashMap::retain internally acquires a write-lock on all shards sequentially — effectively a global lock on the replay set. Under concurrent auth load (PoW makes reconnection expensive, but burst re-auths are still possible), all authenticating tokio tasks serialize through retain(). Impact: O(N) scan on each auth where N can be up to MAX_REPLAY_ENTRIES=1,000,000. The eviction should run on a background timer (tokio::time::interval) rather than inline per-auth, or use a time-bucketed structure (e.g. separate sets per 10-minute window, evict by swapping).
